review_text: Wonderful campsite. Very clean. Showers are solar so they are hot if the sun is shining

This is a place where you stay for 3 nights and wish you stayed for 4, or stay for 4 nights and wish you stayed for 5.

Lovely camping area tucked away in the bush land. Some camp spot are completely covered by trees and others have open sky, so if you require solar antenna view of the sky let the cartaker know and they'll try to accommodate.

The caretaker/host was wonderful, super easy to take with and very helpful. You can buy local honey, eggs and fire wood. The fire wood comes in a bag for $10 and for each night cost $24.

You can either walk down to the beach or rocks, or even drive into the beach and drive as far as you'd like.

I would love to come back one day.

Also some great fishing to be had here. Caught wrasse, whiting, herring and some huge salmon.

This place is simply beautiful, the friendly caretakers Pete & Sharon were lovely. My friend & myself were there on 30th April for 2 days but unfortunately my friend had a slip on the rocks & broke her wrist Sharon & Pete were so helpful to get an ambulance to pick her up & offered so much help with my dogs etc. Thanks Parry Beach we will definitely be back.
Great caravan park

I had the best campsite experience of my entire motorbike road trip here.

After two weeks on the road, this was the first place where I was actually charged less for my motorbike than people with caravans, which I really appreciated. But what truly made this stay special was the kindness and hospitality of the hosts.

A big storm was coming in, and they made sure to warn me ahead of time and even offered me a spot to sleep in a “shed”. That gesture honestly saved my trip. On top of that, the owner organized a campfire in the evening, brought everyone together, and made a real effort to connect with guests. He was incredibly friendly and even invited me for a couple of beers :))

It felt less like a campsite and more like a small community. I’m genuinely grateful for this experience and would highly recommend this place to anyone traveling through the area. The facilities were also in great condition and exactly what you’d expect from a well-run campsite :))
Great camp-site, we regularly have our kids parties here.

Parry Beach Campsite has a fantastic location, but there are a few areas that could really improve the experience. An online booking system would make things much easier, especially during busy periods. Many of the campsites are heavily tree-covered, which means solar access is very limited. Generators are permitted, which we needed to run morning and night—apologies to our neighbours. We found the volunteer interactions to be not overly friendly. With a few small tweaks, this campground could be so much better.

You know what I really love about this place?

No, it's not the absolutely lovely caretakers that were so delightful and helpful when we got there.

No, it's not the fact that it's actually fair priced, which allows us to have a bit of money left over to spend around town, and in the other businesses in the area.

No, it's not that it's cute and really well looked after, and in an amazing location.

It's the fact that there's a pretty low height restriction which means those man child Ram drivers, with embarrassingly huge rigs can't get in.

That means no massive diesel engine sitting there and idling for half an hour while he warms up his toy in the morning.

It means there's no one walking around with small man syndrome puffing their chest out and trying to inflate their own ego.

It means everyone that's in here is in a small pop top, or a camper, or a rooftop tent, and I have just found everybody to be so absolutely pleasant and delightful to talk with this last week.

I've found the nicest people are in little old pop-top vans.

Well, thank you to Parry Beach Campsite.

6/5 stars!

We paid $24 for one night just to sleep in our car in what felt like a random spot. It’s honestly frustrating and hard to justify paying this much for barely two square meters and no proper facilities. In areas like this, you can usually camp for free, so it makes no sense at all to be charged for something so basic. To make things worse, a man came to ask us for money without any kindness or explanation, which left us wondering what service we were actually paying for. There is no drinking water available, and the tap water is yellow, which is very concerning. Really disappointing experience.
Great site run by friendly volunteers.   There is a height restriction of 2.7m on entry due to the low hanging tree branches.  Good value for money.

Low-key, community-run beachside camping for small caravans, camper trailers and tents.
Size limit - no large motor homes over 2.7 m in height (they won’t fit under the branches of the peppermint trees).
Shady and sunny sites available.
No pre-bookings - see the volunteer caretaker on the day you arrive (office hours 9-12am, 3-5pm).
Busy during summer and Easter school holiday periods (limited number of sites so the campsite does get full).
Toilets and showers available (solar hot water - limited supply).
No powered sites - generators allowed (limited hours).
No camp or cooking fires in summer due to local government regulations. Campfires allowed in winter (firewood available for purchase - no collecting firewood).
The woman working there was so sweet and even though we arrived after reception hours she welcomed us and found us a spot. Nice location by the beach, good itinerary with toilets, showers and sinks:)

Lovely rustic bush camp adjacent to Parry Beach. Very peaceful, all you can hear at night is the ocean and the wildlife. Showers and flushing toilets. There are barbecues and sinks for dish washing. No booking system, sites are allocated on a first in basis. It’s very popular so you need to arrive early to try to get a site.
Super nice place perfect to go to the beach close by
Amazing and relaxing spot to camp. Our van too big so we were in overflow. The facilities were great and everyone super friendly. Loved the location for a morning run and swim.

Parry Beach Campsite is delightful! The campsite features a beautiful tree canopy throughout the grounds, the showers + toilets are well maintained/serviced & the fish cleaning area is positioned away from the camp (that is a good thing). The caretakers were really awesome! The campsite is nestled next to a beautiful beach that is accessible by foot & 4-wheel, drive too. This campsite is an absolute hidden gem!!!
Shower was cold and there was no light in the shower
Beautiful spot.
Lovely people and beats beach! Solar panels for hot water so might take cold showers
A really nice camping ground that works on a first in best dressed basis. Sue the caretaker is so lovely and very helpful. We would be more than happy to visit again.
Amazing campground, well run and plenty of space. Van must be under 2.7m to get in. March flies were a pest!

Absolutely love coming here ❤️ my partner and I come twice a year.
Amenities are clean and well looked after by volunteers.
Such a fantastic spot and camp spot.
See you in a couple days!!
2nd campaign trip for the year coming up.

Parrys beach is on my opinion only good for launching boats and swimming with family and the occasional surfing there are no good spots for fishing anything you get is micro but nice long beach for kids and family

Excellent camping.
Well cared for facilities.
Get there early to guarantee your spot.
Parry beach is the best stayed a week pet friendly great fishing has showers toilets great swimming awesome area good Bush walk's.
Caretakers are very helpful,  welcoming and friendly. Sites and campground are very neat and spacious. Quiet and peaceful 😊

The Parry Beach Campsite was a wonderful camping experience! Not too pricey and very fair concerning the amenities the campsite has to offer. The toilettes and showers were very clean, the camping area was beneath beautiful trees that were giving the campground a nice charming atmosphere. Each campsite has their own fireplace, you can buy the wood at the office. (Only cash) You cannot book the campground in advance tho, but that didn’t bother us. Would recommend this Campsite!

Nice little spot set amongst trees, beach just at the door.  There are showers but they are solar, so a bit hit n miss withe this rainy overcast days. Close to all the sightseeing.  Lovely host. Just remember no big  caravans because of trees. We took out 17ft pop top in.

We highly recommend Parry Beach Campsite because on arrival we were greeted by Lynette who is the friendliest caretaker that made us feel welcome. The amenities are old but very clean. Now please note = that caretaker and cleaners are volunteers because they care about the campsite. Check your van height as if you are over 2.7 metres high you will not fit as it is a long way to drive to be turned away. Now as for Josh B comment from 3 weeks ago,so if he wasn’t happy with this place he needs to give up camping. To say hurtful things about someone who loves what she is doing is wrong.
Thank you for reading and i hope you have a great stay like we did.

Amazing spot, camping to cater all types, full beach access for cars, small boats, or juat walk ur dog.
Has a full time caretaker, OMG, what a job to get to this beautiful place, very helpful and friendly.
Definitely recommend this on ur travels

In tegenstelling tot andere reviews een goede ervaring gehad met de campsite en beheerder. De beheerder was ontzettend vriendelijk en meedenkend en de campsite was prachtig gelegen. De ongelofelijke herrie van de generatoren is het enige nadeel (gelukkig maar beperkt toegestaan).
Caretaker was very rude. Felt better moving on to another place. Maybe back off the sardines.
Cheap campsite with access to a beautiful beach. Dog friendly and run by helpful volunteers. Two wheel drive accessible.

Back to nature. No power or water but toilets/showers and basic washing up facilities all using bore water. Pretty much right on the beach bush camping. Heaps of sites.
Very helpful and friendly staff. We paid $23.50 a night on a concession. That's for two of us.

Very clean camp with spotless toilets and solar showers. Caretaker was lovely offering to let me pick out which spot I wanted as I didn’t want to be packed in by other campers. No bookings, first in best dressed. Cash or card payments. We stayed midweek but reckon it could get busy at weekends/holidays.
Great relaxing place clean facilities volunteer caretakers are great

If you are requiring an answer to a question, don’t hold your breath! Pretty sure they were only capable of responding to the bottom line of and email.
Nothing but rude and incompetent to deal with.

Carried out by volunteers, they are great and kind people. The place is surrounded by nature and is peaceful, next to the beach where you can swim and fish. There are hot showers, powered and unpowered sites for 23 dollars. I would be happy to stop by again.
Lovely campground in the forest and close to the beach. Perfect for kids. Only $23 per night with hot shower.

Slept here in a car with Rooftop tent, there is an overflow campsite if campground is full.
Clean facilities and welcoming staff !

Llegamos ahí y justo había un puesto disponible, la playa no es muy bonita pero el camping es lo máximo! Los espacios son amplios y lo suficientemente separados entre sí. Llenos de naturaleza, también tienen baños con ducha caliente!
great little Camp ground clean toilets and showers friendly staff and good fishing

Absolutely beautiful. Caretakers are lovely, facilities are standard but good for camp-ground. Bore water for tap and showers. So have to be prepared with own drinking water or boiling bore water.
The beach access is convenient and easy to get too.
What an amazing place to spend a few days.
Magic.
Nice spot if you have a small van no good if you have a tinny on the ute.stayed in the small overflow space but not level.bit rich for $23 per night I feel

The caretakers attitude is absolutely disgusting!
The camp ground and location is stunning, even when it's cold and windy. There are so many things to do and places to see here, it really is a beautiful place to visit.
There are 2 toilet/shower blocks at the camp grounds. The one located in the middle of the park, had the male and female blocks locked off on our 2nd day, yet the disabled one was left open to use. On our 3rd afternoon, I went for a shower in there and had the door rattled on me as the caretaker wanted to lock that toilet/shower off too. I naturally questioned to confirm that the whole block would then be locked off, to which she replied yes. She said she would return to lock the toilet/shower. Instead, sat on her quad bike outside saying to herself (but loud enough for me to hear) come on! She then rattled the door not once but twice again. 3 times in total! She knew I was in there so why not come back! I'm a paying guest having a shower that I've paid for! Plus it's a Friday of the public holiday weekend, so why lock that block off.
Anyway, hubby went to inform her of how upset I was and she said sorry. It is what it is.
Later, Hubby saw the caretaker again (after she had been to every site EXCEPT OURS to offer firewood) and asked if she could re-open the block as it's pretty busy with campers. The caretaker proceeded to tell him that she had no cleaners, so wouldn't open it. He volunteered to clean the toilet/shower blocks for a nights free stay, to which she replied 'you're leaving tomorrow'. My hubbys response was 'It is what it is.
Unbeknown to her, we were talking about staying longer, as some campers do extend their stays, which we had already done once. Yet due to her disgusting attitude, we don't feel welcome here at all.
The volunteers that work here are amazing. They came to our aid after a tree branch hit our van (caretaker nowhere to be seen). I cannot thank them enough for their amazing hospitality.
If the caretaker hates her job so much, then leave. We've ran holiday parks before and know what it can be like, but the way you treated us.... Wow!

Great spot!! Lots of sites (40+), nicely spaced with shade and trees. $23/night for two people in a tent, firewood available from the caretaker $10/bag. Dog friendly. Joins up with the Bib Track.

Good facilities (toilet blocks, solar showers, bins, non-potable water, sinks). Managed to get a site on a Friday arvo of a long weekend.

Note: Caretaker sign says no enquiries after 5pm but was told the next day we should have still checked in even though we arrived after. Worth a knock to check if anyone's in!

Great place to camp, the beach access and beach is amazing. Great for fishing and relaxing.
Wonderful service from the temporary caretaker.

Will definitely visit again.
Excellent hospitality and brilliant location. Very memorable spot to pitch a tent, stargaze and to sit by a fire.
Amazing place

Great spot. The caretakers, Lynette and Trixie were wonderful to deal with. No power or water. Have solar powered showers, so get in early. This little camp spot is gorgeous with access to the beach and a nice drive to all the tourist attractions. We loved it. Not suitable for caravans over 2.7m high.
Peace and quiet perfect place for taking easy

I had the pleasure of hosting this amazing place for 14 days over Easter and wow .. best if you are well prepared for non powered sites , so quiet  and peaceful , showers (run on solar) and toilets either side of the camp,  non bookable so get the in the morning meet the most amazing people ,, well done parrys the volunteers are fantastic
Very nice to bib walkers

Able to drive onto the beach as it is rock hard.
No vacancies in camping area, and overflow car park was being used by big vans that were too big to enter camp ground.
Photo taken a short walk or by 4wd from the camp ground.

A gorgeous camping spot, felt very lucky staying even for just one night. There’s no clean water or kitchen area but it’s a wonderful spot camping under the trees and being so close to the beach. Thank you to the volunteers who run it!!

Wonderful camp spot. There is no drinking water and the water from the taps didn’t run clear so we didn’t shower, but the scenery and location were wonderful. Friendly staff, affordable and hassle free. Would stay again
Very friendly campsite in beautiful surroundings. Lady in the caretaker’s cottage went above and beyond to find us a space on a full site. Check out Green Pool nearby, stunning spot.
Nice beach side campsite. Sites a little small compared to other sites but still worth the journey.
Lovely spot within a stones throw of the most stunning beach!
Don't tell anyone about this place keep this little gem a secret. Easily the best spot we have stopped at this trip. Love Parry Beach.
Parry beach is the best place for a get away in nature.
Rude Care taker. Overpriced for a camp site that is too cramped, no reception, yellow water when you take a shower,

Parrys beach is a must see destination from salmon season to fishing, Parry's beach has so much to offer. With a 24hr caretaker it's amazing. Showers and toilets provided, day trips welcomed and the coast is stunning. A hidden gem
Beautiful camping with all you need,showers,bathroom,barbecue and a nice beach just in front of it!

My favourite camping spot! We had a fantastic time and stayed longer than planned. The Parry ladies are fantastic, the place is beautiful - camping in the forest with heaps of shade right next to a stunning beach. Toilets and showers were very clean. It's run by solar so hot water may run out if you leave it late. Short 20min drive to Denmark town which is charming and had everything we could need. Also really close to other must see places such as the elephant rock and green pools.

Great place to camp and lovely beaches. Toilets are clean and tidy. Good job to the volunteers that maintain the place. Pity about load music from fellow campers. It spoils the tranquillity of the place.

A lot of work is going into looking after this campground and managing it well for the enduring use of visitors. The host was very helpful to us when we asked for a site with sun for a couple of days. We stayed 2 extra nights beyond our original booking. Thanks.
Amazing place summer winter all seasons
Beautiful place to just unwind. Lots of great people with their pets as that’s allowed. Simple but great

Run purely by volunteers who do an awesome job. Campers can also volunteer in return for free camping when needs demand.
Lovely spot,bushy campsites for 1 or 2 vehicles depending on size. Large caravans are not able to be accommodated.
Can drive on the beach which is also walking distance. All in all a delightful experience.
Solar showers...just about adequate...dogs are permitted..
Great place but gets very busy

Absolutely lovely, nice clean n tidy camp grounds, minute walk to beach, Absolutely stunning.
Would definitely recommend this place.

Caretaker is rude and obnoxious. Never had an issue picking a site that suits our setup up til the recent caretaker. They allocate a site and won't listen to reason when you ask to move to another spot. They bunch everyone together like sardines...
This place used to be a stress free getaway and now it's just another unpleasantly run military camp.
Denmark shire you need to address this and make it what it used to be. Been visiting for 15yrs and it's never been this bad.

I've been camping at Parry's for over 10 years and never had a problem.  The last caretaker 2023 was the rudest most arrogant person I've met.  His wife was no better and abused myself and my friends.  He should not be allowed to care take when he has soooo many personal issues and takes them out on the campers

Very beautiful campsite, the staff are lovely, lots of cute wildlife in the area!!! The facilities are well maintained, however please be aware that you will not always get hot water here! Bring backup options for bathing just in case!! Additionally, everything runs on bore water, I am unsure/can’t remember if there is a drinking water facility here: ensure you bring fresh water if you don’t want to drink boiled bore water!!! I’ve been in both Winter (recently) and Summer - definitely busier in summer but honestly, if you have good timing with the weather, winter is perfect, a lot quieter! This campsite gets a 10/10 from me and I will 100% be planning to come back another time :)
Wish I could leave no stars!  caretakers is rude and unprofessional.   Been coming here for years and will never come again.
Awesome spot,  great facilities, and friendly, helpful staff. Will definitely be coming back!
Great caretakers. Loos and showers are great . Solar shower . Clean and spacious  sites. Dog friendly  and easy access  to the beach to go for a walk  or drive.  Even better you can have a camp fire
Nice spot but caretaker completely rude and unfriendly. Karen on steroids.
Best camping experience of my life

An amazing well-priced campsite which with close accessibility to the beach access for activities, 4WD and fishing, and friendly volunteer campsite caretakers.

Amenities are well-kept (regular toilets available) and hot water heated by solar panels for showers.

Note that the second half of the beach transitions into soft sand, so ensure you have appropriate recovery gear available if you get bogged. Tides can come in high.

We spent 2 nights here, truly a lovely spot, caretaker is lovely and the volunteers are friendly and welcoming. Amenities are clean. Walk to lookout amazing, lucky to see a whale, could just sit and watch the ocean all day. Well worth a visit, will come back one day for sure.
